programme.desc: Na een teleurstellend einde van 2021 verwelkomden de All Blacks in juli het nummer één team ter wereld, Ierland, voor een reeks van drie tests. Het team werd op en naast het veld uitgedaagd, wat een ongekende verandering in het trainingskamp teweegbracht. Het team reisde af naar Zuid-Afrika om te vechten voor trots- en voor elkaar.

programme.title: Inside Ajax
programme.desc: Ter ere van de 500ste Europese wedstrijd tegen Inter blikken we in deze special terug op de rijke Europese historie van de club. Van het debuut achter het IJzeren Gordijn en de legendarische trilogie in de jaren 70, tot de Champions League-triomf in Wenen en de galavoorstelling in Bernabéu. Een halve eeuw Europese magie in vogelvlucht.

programme.title: Ajax wint de Wereldcup
programme.desc: Documentaire waarin wordt teruggeblikt op 1995, het jaar waarin Ajax de wereldbeker won. De beelden van de feestende Ajacieden in het stadion en in het hotel, de interviews, de wereldbeker, de reis terug en de huldiging in Amsterdam staan centraal.

programme.title: KICK 't MET
programme.desc: Kenneth Taylor blikt openhartig terug op zijn overstap van zijn vertrouwde 'warm bad' bij Ajax naar Serie A-topclub Lazio Roma. Hij bespreekt de tactische cultuurschok in Italië, het uiteenvallen van zijn Amsterdamse vriendengroep en de waardevolle lessen die hij meekreeg van Francesco Farioli. Tot slot laat de ambitieuze middenvelder er geen misverstand over bestaan: een toekomstige terugkeer naar zijn grote liefde Ajax sluit hij absoluut niet uit.

programme.title: Wes & Raf
programme.desc: In aflevering 3 van Wes & Raf blikken Wesley Sneijder en Rafael van der Vaart samen met Noa Vahle terug op hun gezamenlijke jaren in het Nederlands elftal. Van onvergetelijke hoogtepunten op eindtoernooien tot de teleurstellingen die Oranje ook kende: alles komt voorbij. Hoe bijzonder is het om het Oranje-shirt te dragen en je land te vertegenwoordigen op het hoogste podium? En hoe kon het dat Oranje na het magische WK van 2010 twee jaar later zo teleurstellend presteerde op het EK van 2012?

programme.title: Frank Slotta maakt kennis met rugby: Schotland - Frankrijk in de Six Nations
programme.desc: Frank Slotta had nog nooit een rugbywedstrijd gezien: hoog tijd dus voor Jay-Jay Boske om hem mee te nemen! En niet zomaar een wedstrijd: in Schotland strijdt het thuisland tegen titelverdediger Frankrijk in de Six Nations. Wint Schotland? Dan ligt alles open voor de laatste speelronde, met nog 3 titelkandidaten. Wint Frankrijk? Dan kan het de titel prolongeren… Slotta laat zich onderdompelen in alles wat rugby zo'n mooie, unieke sport maakt.

programme.title: Chronicles of a Champion Golfer...
programme.desc: A look at one of The Open's great champions. Here, the focus is on American Phil Mickelson, who won his sole Championship in 2013 at Muirfield Golf Links.

programme.title: The 154th Open: Round 1 Highlights
programme.desc: The opening round of the 154th Open Championship, staged at Royal Birkdale. A year on from Scottie Scheffler's Portrush masterclass, the Claret Jug is up for grabs once more. (16.07)

programme.title: The Open Films: Harrington 2008
programme.desc: Sky Sports looks back at some of the best contests at The Open. Here, the focus is on The 2008 Open at Royal Birkdale - Padraig Harrington returned as the defending champion from 2007.

programme.title: The Open Films: Oosthuizen 2010
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best contests at The Open. Here, the focus is on The 2010 Open on the Old Course at St Andrews, which saw a South African golfer claim his first Major.

programme.title: The Open Films: Clarke 2011
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best contests at The Open. Here, a look at The 2011 Open at Royal St George's Golf Club, as a Northern Irish golfer enjoyed a memorable week.

programme.title: Chronicles Of A Champion Golfer Padraig Harrington
programme.desc: A look at one of The Open's great champions. Here, the focus is on Irishman Padraig Harrington, a two-time Open winner who took back-to-back titles in 2007 and 2008.

programme.title: Live The Open: Round 2
programme.desc: The second round of the 154th Open Championship, staged at Royal Birkdale.

programme.title: The Open: Round 2 Highlights
programme.desc: The second round of the 154th Open Championship, staged at Royal Birkdale. (17.07)

programme.title: Chronicles Of A Champion Golfer Tiger Woods
programme.desc: A compelling documentary in which Woods talks about his 20-year involvement in The Open. Tiger also looks at the impact of his father's death and what the future holds.

programme.title: The Open Films: Baker Finch 1991
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best contests at The Open in years gone by. Here, The 1991 Open at Royal Birkdale, where a surprise champion would win his sole Major.

programme.title: Chronicles Of A Champion Golfer Greg Norman
programme.desc: Born down under, Greg Norman was twice Open Champion and dominated golf for a decade. Always charismatic, at times controversial, he won 91 titles worldwide.

programme.title: Chronicles Of A Champion Golfer Ernie Els
programme.desc: A look at the career of Ernie Els - the South African golfer has been a major winner four times - twice at the US Open and twice at The Open - across a glittering career.

programme.title: The 154th Open: Round 2 Highlights
programme.desc: The second round of the 154th Open Championship, staged at Royal Birkdale. (17.07)

programme.title: The Open Films: Els 2012
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best contests at The Open. Here, a look at The 2012 Open at Royal Lytham & St Annes Golf Club, a tournament that went right down to the wire in the final round.

programme.title: The Open Films: Mickelson 2013
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best contests at The Open in years gone by. Here, a look at The 2013 Open at Muirfield, which concluded with a memorable final round comeback.

programme.title: The Open Films: Johnson 2015
programme.desc: The official film of The 2015 Open Championship, held on the Old Course at St Andrews. It would be a memorable week in Scotland for a former Masters winner.

programme.title: The Open Films: McIlroy 2014
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best contests at The Open in years gone by. This episode focuses on the 2014 tournament, held at the Royal Liverpool Golf Club.

programme.title: The Open: Round 1 Highlights
programme.desc: The opening round of the 154th Open Championship, staged at Royal Birkdale. A year on from Scottie Scheffler's Portrush masterclass, the Claret Jug is up for grabs once more. (16.07)

programme.title: The Open: Round 2 Highlights
programme.desc: The second round of the 154th Open Championship, staged at Royal Birkdale. (17.07)

programme.title: Live: Saturday At The Open
programme.desc: On Saturday at the 154th Open Championship, the third round at Royal Birkdale awaits. Look ahead to all the play on an action-packed day three of the age-old competition.

programme.title: Live The Open: Round 3
programme.desc: The third round of the 154th Open Championship, staged at Royal Birkdale.

programme.title: The Open: Round 3 Highlights
programme.desc: The third round of the 154th Open Championship, staged at Royal Birkdale. (18.07)

programme.title: Justin Rose: Chasing Forever
programme.desc: Chasing the ultimate dream of lifting the Claret Jug, this is Justin Rose's story. Told through four decades of tournament footage, this documentary unpacks his defining moments.

programme.title: This Is Open Country
programme.desc: Charting Irish success at The Open, Rory McIlroy, Shane Lowry, Pádraig Harrington and Darren Clarke recount their personal journeys to the top and relive the moment victory was realised.

programme.title: Underdogs: The Untold Story of the 138th Open
programme.desc: A putt away from what would've been the most astonishing underdog triumph ever, this is the story of Tom Watson and the near-miraculous moment that cruelly evaded him.

programme.title: FNF: Bournemouth v Man Utd
programme.desc: With daylight between them and Villa, Manchester United's fight for Champions League qualification continues on Friday Night Football with a trip to Bournemouth's Vitality Stadium. (20.03)

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: Four games without a win and nine points away from safety, relegation-threatened Burnley need a result here as Fulham - who also drew 0-0 last week - visit Turf Moor. (21.03)

programme.title: SNF: Everton v Chelsea Hlts
programme.desc: After a midweek European exit, Chelsea's attention turns to qualification again as the Blues head to the Hill Dickinson Stadium for a clash with fellow European-hopefuls Everton. (21.03)

programme.title: SNF: Leeds v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: On Saturday Night Football at Elland Road, Leeds United - three points above the Premier League's drop zone - play host to a Keith Andrews Brentford side dreaming of Europe. (21.03)

programme.title: PL: Newcastle v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: A St James' Park Tyne-Wear derby on Super Sunday sees local rivals Newcastle and Sunderland collide in the Premier League. The two teams are separated by just two points. (22.03)

programme.title: PL: Aston Villa v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: Climbing out of the bottom three for the first time since December last weekend, can West Ham pull clear again as Nuno Espirito Santo's men visit Villa on Super Sunday? (22.03)

programme.title: PL: Spurs v Nott'm Forest Hlts
programme.desc: On a huge afternoon at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, a desperate Spurs take on relegation rivals Nottingham Forest in the Premier League on Super Sunday. (22.03)

programme.title: FNF: West Ham v Wolves Hlts
programme.desc: A point from safety, Nuno Espirito Santo's West Ham welcome bottom-of-the-table Wolves to the London Stadium. Rob Edwards' visitors have won two of their last three. (10.04)

programme.title: PL: Brentford v Everton Hlts
programme.desc: Seventh meets eighth in the race for Europe as Brentford - held to three consecutive Premier League draws - entertain David Moyes' Everton at the Gtech Community Stadium. (11.04)

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Brighton Hlts
programme.desc: Fabian Hurzeler's Brighton, who prior to the international break collected back-to-back wins, resume their bid for European football next season with a trip to Burnley's Turf Moor. (11.04)

programme.title: PL: C. Palace v Newcastle Hlts
programme.desc: Crystal Palace and Newcastle meet at Selhurst Park on Super Sunday. Three Premier League points separates the hosts from the Magpies, with Palace also holding a game in hand. (12.04)

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v A. Villa Hlts
programme.desc: With both sides picking up much-needed wins before the break, Nottingham Forest's fight for survival resumes against an Aston Villa side chasing Champions League qualification. (12.04)

programme.title: SNF: Liverpool v Fulham Hlts
programme.desc: With one route back into the Champions League virtually closed midweek, can Arne Slot's Liverpool prise open another as they host Fulham in their bid to secure a top-five finish? (11.04)

programme.title: PL: Sunderland v Spurs Hlts
programme.desc: The Roberto De Zerbi era begins for Tottenham Hotspur. With games running out, Spurs are desperately chasing a result away to Regis Le Bris' European outsiders Sunderland. (12.04)

programme.title: PL: Chelsea v Man City Hlts
programme.desc: The Carabao Cup winners Manchester City, who booked another trip to Wembley last weekend, go to Chelsea on Super Sunday bidding to keep touch with Premier League leaders Arsenal. (12.04)

programme.title: MNF: Man Utd v Leeds Utd Hlts
programme.desc: One of England's most famous rivalries is renewed on Monday Night Football as Michael Carrick's Champions League-chasing Manchester United host FA Cup semi-finalists Leeds. (13.04)

programme.title: EFL Cup Best Goals: 2024/25
programme.desc: From the Valley all the way to Wembley - this season's Carabao Cup has thrown up a host of stunning goals. Look back at the finest strikes from the 2024-25 competition.

programme.title: EFL Trophy Final 2024/25
programme.desc: In the final of the 2024-2025 EFL Trophy, Birmingham City and Peterborough compete at Wembley Stadium. (13.04)

programme.title: EFL League 1 Play-Off Final: 19/20
programme.desc: With a place in the Sky Bet Championship on the line, the two League One play-off finalists go head-to-head at Wembley Stadium.

programme.title: EFL C'Ship Play-Off Final: 19/20
programme.desc: West London sides collide in the Sky Bet Championship play-off final at Wembley as Brentford and Fulham go head-to-head for the final place in next season's Premier League.

programme.title: SPFL 24/25: Rangers v Celtic
programme.desc: The champions and runaway leaders Celtic travel to face Rangers at Ibrox in the Scottish Premiership for the year's first Old Firm derby. A massive 14 points separate the two sides. (02.01)

programme.title: EFL 17/18: Bristol City v Man Utd
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the EFL Cup. Here, Bristol City host Premier League Man Utd at Ashton Gate in the 2017-18 competition's quarter-finals.

programme.title: EFL 17/18: Leeds v Bristol City
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the EFL. Here, Leeds United host Bristol City at Elland Road in the Sky Bet Championship in a match that saw a dramatic comeback.

programme.title: Retro EFL Cup 13: Bradford v Swans
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic League Cup final from years gone, as Swansea City and League One Bradford City go head-to-head in the 2012-13 campaign for the season's first silverware.

programme.title: Retro C'Ship 10: B'pool v Cardiff
programme.desc: A chance to relive the memorable 2010 Championship play-off final, as Blackpool and Cardiff City go head-to-head at Wembley in a thrilling final with a lot on the line.

programme.title: Celtic's Invincibles
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes of Celtic's remarkable 2016-2017 campaign in the SPFL Premiership, in which they completed their title-winning season without losing a league match.

programme.title: SPFL 19/20: Rangers v Celtic
programme.desc: It's the first Old Firm derby of the season as Rangers take on Celtic at Ibrox in the SPFL Ladbrokes Premiership. Rangers won 2-0 in the last meeting of the two sides last year.

programme.title: EFL 17/18: Arsenal v Man City
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the EFL Cup. Here, Arsenal and Man City go head-to-head in the final of the 2017-18 competition at Wembley.

programme.title: EFL 17/18: Aston Villa v Wolves
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the EFL. Here, Aston Villa host Wolves at Villa Park in a memorable Midlands derby in the 2017-18 campaign.

programme.title: EFL Cup 12/13: Bradford v Swansea
programme.desc: A look back at some famous matches from yesteryear. Here, Bradford City take on Swansea City in the final of the Capital One Cup at Wembley Stadium in 2013.

programme.title: SPFL 24/25: Hibernian v Hearts
programme.desc: The Scottish Premiership's bottom two - Hibs and Hearts - do battle at Easter Road. After a dominant outing against St Mirren, can Neil Critchley command another strong performance? (27.10)

programme.title: SPFL 24/25: Hearts v Hibernian
programme.desc: A Scottish Premiership Edinburgh derby on Boxing Day sees Hearts face Hibs at Tynecastle Park. Can Jordan Smith's side break their five-game winless streak over their fierce rivals? (26.12)

programme.title: EFL 17/18: Fulham v QPR
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the EFL. Here, promotion-chasing Fulham host QPR at Craven Cottage in a west London derby that saw a memorable comeback.

programme.title: EFL 17/18: Middlesbrough v Wolves
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the EFL. Here, Middlesbrough host Wolves at the Riverside in 2018 in a game that saw the visitors reduced to nine men.

programme.title: EFL Play-Off Final: C'Ship 07/08
programme.desc: A look back at some classic Football League play-off finals from years gone by. Here, Bristol City take on Hull City at Wembley in the Championship play-off final in 2008.

programme.title: EFL Play-Off Final: C'Ship 08/09
programme.desc: A look back at some classic Football League play-off finals from years gone by. Here, Burnley take on Sheffield United at Wembley in the Championship play-off final in 2009.

programme.title: EFL Play-Off Final: C'Ship 11/12
programme.desc: A look back at some classic Football League play-off finals from years gone by. Here, Blackpool take on West Ham United at Wembley in the Championship play-off final in 2012.

programme.title: SPFL 22/23: Celtic v Rangers
programme.desc: The quest for Glasgow supremacy starts here, as Celtic and Rangers contest the season's first Old Firm at Celtic Park. Who will land the first blow in this storied rivalry? (03.09)

programme.title: EFL 16/17: Leeds Utd v Aston Villa
programme.desc: Relive some classic matches in English football. Here, Leeds United face Aston Villa in the Sky Bet Championship, with the sides meeting in the league for the first time since 2004.

programme.title: EFL 16/17: Huddersfield v Leeds
programme.desc: Relive some classic matches in English football. Here, a West Yorkshire derby as Huddersfield Town play Leeds United in 2017 in the Sky Bet Championship.

programme.title: EFL Cup 22/23: Man Utd v Newcastle
programme.desc: Twenty four years in the making, Newcastle return to Wembley for a Carabao Cup final with Manchester United, as Erik ten Hag has the chance to end United's six-year trophy drought. (26.02)

programme.title: EFL Cup Best Goals: 2022/23
programme.desc: From the Abbey all the way to Wembley - this season's Carabao Cup has thrown up a host of spectacular goals. Look back at the 2022-2023 competition's finest strikes.

programme.title: EFL Trophy Final 2022/23
programme.desc: The moment to shine at Wembley has arrived for Bolton Wanderers and Plymouth Argyle, as the pair turn their attentions away from promotion to the final of the 2022-23 EFL Trophy. (02.04)

programme.title: EFL Play-Off Final: C'Ship 09/10
programme.desc: A look back at some classic Football League play-off finals from years gone by. Here, Blackpool take on Cardiff City at Wembley in the Championship play-off final in 2010.

programme.title: EFL Play-Off Final: C'Ship 10/11
programme.desc: A look back at some classic Football League play-off finals from years gone by. Here, Reading take on Swansea City at Wembley in the Championship play-off final in 2011.

programme.title: The Story of Frank Soo
programme.desc: Frank Soo was the first player of Asian heritage to play for England, featuring nine times during WWII. Recognised as a trailblazer, his family received a posthumous cap in his honour.

programme.title: SPFL 22/23: Rangers v Celtic
programme.desc: Ibrox readies itself for Scotland's biggest fixture - the Old Firm derby. Separated by nine Scottish Premiership points, can Rangers do anything to stop Ange Postecoglou's Celtic? (02.01)

programme.title: EFL 16/17: Sheff Weds v Newcastle
programme.desc: Relive some classic matches in English football. Here, Sheffield Wednesday take on Newcastle United in 2017 in the Sky Bet Championship, with both sides looking for promotion chances.

programme.title: EFL 16/17: Fulham v Aston Villa
programme.desc: Remember some classic matches from English football. In this episode, Fulham looked to maintain their playoff hopes against Aston Villa in the Sky Bet Championship in April 2017.

programme.title: EFL Cup 23/24: Chelsea v Liverpool
programme.desc: Jurgen Klopp's farewell tour stops at Wembley for this season's Carabao Cup final as Liverpool face Chelsea. Just two years ago, the Reds beat the Blues here for a ninth League Cup. (25.02)
